{{Quote|It may appear to some that the enactment of universal laws and the widespread deployment of a heavily armed military are steps toward galactic domination, but these actions are taken merely to protect us from those who would invade, enslave, exploit, or foment political dissent, and to punish accordingly any who engage in such acts. Look on our new military not as trespassers or interlopers, but as gatekeepers, here to shore up the Emperor's vision of a pacified and prosperous galaxy.|[[Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin]], in his Tarkin Doctrine|<ref name="Tarkin" />}}
The '''Imperial Military''', also referred to as the '''Imperial Armed Forces''', comprised the various branches of the armed forces of the [[Galactic Empire]], such as the [[Imperial Army]] and [[Imperial Navy]] and the [[Stormtrooper Corps]], as well as agencies such as [[Imperial Intelligence]] aiding the Imperial Military. 

As part of the realignment of the [[Republic Military]] following the [[Clone Wars]] and the [[Proclamation of the New Order|transformation]] from [[Galactic Republic]] to the Empire, the nascent Imperial Military went under a massive militarization effort and was tasked with enforcement across the new Empire and was used for suppressing [[planet]]ary uprisings, continuing the [[Great Jedi Purge]] and chasing [[Separatist holdouts|holdouts]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]].

However, it would be faced with a [[Early rebellion against the Galactic Empire|growing rebellion]] which caused concern within the military, ultimately resulting in the formation of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Civil War]]. The Imperial [[stormtrooper]]s and [[Star Destroyer]]s were deployed and fought across the galaxy to retain control. Even after the [[Battle of Yavin|loss]] of the [[Death Star]] over [[Yavin]], the Empire was able to almost defeat the rebels during the [[Battle of Hoth]]. However, the Alliance [[Unity Day attack|endured]] and continued it effort. Six months later, the Imperial Military suffered a sudden and crushing defeat at the [[Battle of Endor]].

After successive defeats by [[New Republic]] as well as the power vacuum left in the wake of the [[Emperor]]'s death following [[Endor]], the Imperial Military ultimately [[Battle of Jakku|fell]] with the Empire and broke into various [[Imperial Remnants|remnants]], where they awaited the return of [[Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo|Thrawn]] and a reemergence of the Imperial Military. While the [[First Order]], [[Contingency|designed]] from [[Unknown Regions Imperial remnant|elements]] that fled into the [[Unknown Regions]], built an entirely [[First Order military|new military force]].

==History==
===Formation===
{{Quote|Twenty years ago, the galaxy was at war. People lived in fear that fighting would come to their planet or that a terrorist attack would strike near their home. For Republic citizens, seeing white-armored troopers and warships overhead meant they were safe. Today, people who remember those dark days see the arrival of stormtroopers and Imperial Star Destroyers the same way.|General [[Airen Cracken]] of the Rebel Alliance|<ref name="Rebel Dossier">''[[Star Wars: Rogue One: Rebel Dossier]]''</ref>}}
Following the end of the destructive three-year<ref name="Catalyst">''[[Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel]]''</ref> galaxy-wide conflict known as the [[Clone Wars]], then [[Supreme Chancellor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]] ushered in the formation of the [[Galactic Empire]], a [[dictatorship]] composed of the entirety of the former [[Galactic Republic]] and with himself as its [[Emperor|head of state]], the first [[Galactic Emperor]].<ref name="Episode III">{{Film|III}}</ref> With vast portions of the galaxy still unconquered and outside Imperial dominion,<ref name="Tarkin" /> Emperor Palpatine transformed the [[Grand Army of the Republic]] into the Imperial Military composed of both the Army and [[Republic Navy|Navy]] of the prior government.<ref name="Episode III" /> Without the [[Jedi]] present as military leaders, [[officer]]s like [[Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin]] had much more free rein to employ ruthless tactics.<ref name="Tarkin" />

[[File:BadBatchImperialArmy.png|thumb|left|300px|Formed from the force that fought for the Republic, the early Imperial Military made use of clones and Republic equipment.]]
The new and expanded military force was created to reinforce the [[clone trooper]]s of the former [[Republic Military]], as the [[Cloning|clones]] had a limited amount of time they could be in service<ref name="Catalyst" /> due to their accelerated aging, and to ensure Imperial rule. Embarking on a massive militarization effort in order to combat the remaining [[Separatist holdouts]] across the galaxy, as well as bring more worlds under Imperial control, the Imperial Military would serve as the primary means of Imperial enforcement and expansion from the [[Coruscant|Imperial Center]].<ref name="Tarkin" /> Beginning [[Project War-Mantle]] in the first days of the Imperial Era, the Empire would gradually replace the clone troopers over the years with enlisted soldiers due to the cheaper cost<ref name="Aftermath ep">{{TBB|Aftermath (episode)}}</ref> and over concerns that the clones still held loyalty to the former Republic they were made for instead of the [[New Order]],<ref name="War-Mantle">{{TBB|War-Mantle}}</ref> with Tarkin in specific being concerned about the level of individuality the clones had learned from their Jedi officers.<ref name="The Summit">{{TBB|The Summit}}</ref>

===Galactic Civil War===
{{Quote|The mining firms are corrupt. They're tied up—ownership, boards of directors—with the shipwrights that have sold the Empire on one construction project after another. Oh, it's all being done in secret, but you can't keep everything secret. A billion Star Destroyers isn't enough. They're building Super Star Destroyers, and Super-Super Star Destroyers, and who knows what else!|Clone Wars veteran [[Skelly]], referring to the [[thorilide]] triangle theory|<ref name="A New Dawn (novel)">[[A New Dawn (novel)|''A New Dawn'']]</ref>}}
[[File:DarkInk-DisciplesoftheEmpire.png|thumb|right|300px|Conscripted and willingly recruited civilians filled the ranks of the Imperial Military as soldiers and pilots of the Galactic Empire.]]
In the nineteen years following the Empire's rise, a growing rebellion would cause concern within the higher echelons of the Empire, ultimately resulting in the formation of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. For years, the opposing sides fought across the galaxy for control of [[sector]]s, [[Star system|systems]] and the loyalties of their respective populations, with major encounters on the [[Outer Rim Territories|Outer Rim]] worlds of [[Yavin 4]], [[Hoth]], and [[Endor]]. Despite crushing the Alliance on Hoth,<ref name="Episode V" /> the [[Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> and [[Battle of Endor|Endor]]<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> would see the destruction of both [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Stars]],<ref name="ANH" /><ref name="ROTJ" /> following which the Empire would enter into a state of disarray as former military leaders formed splinter fleets, while the [[New Republic]] slowly pushed Imperial forces back towards the [[Core Worlds|Coruscant Core]].<ref name="Aftermath">[[Aftermath (novel)|''Aftermath'']]</ref>

===Disbanding===
[[File:InflictorBoardingParty-LSManga.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Imperial Military made a final stand at the Battle of Jakku, where the New Republic triumphed.]]
Following successive defeats at the hands of the New Republic, the scattered Imperial Military made their [[Battle of Jakku|final stand]] over the world of [[Jakku]]. Suffering heavy casualties, [[Grand Vizier]] [[Mas Amedda]]<ref name="Empire's End">''[[Aftermath: Empire's End]]''</ref> ultimately signed a peace treaty—the [[Galactic Concordance]]—with the New Republic, ending both the battle and the war.<ref name="TFA VD">''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>

The Galactic Empire was to abandon its [[Imperial Academy|Imperial Academies]], with the recruitment and mobilization of [[stormtrooper]] forces is forbidden and was placed with strict disarmament treaties and punishing reparations on the remnants of the Empire.<ref name="TFA VD" /> Large portions of the Empire were incorporated into the New Republic,<ref name="Propaganda">''[[Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy]]''</ref> and the Empire itself formally dissolved as per treaty, having already mostly splintered into various remnants,<ref name="Lost Stars" /> with the Empire having been replaced by the New Republic as its successor.<ref name="Part Three">{{Ahsoka|Part Three: Time to Fly}}</ref>

===Legacy===
With the Empire defeated, its military vehicles were dismantled for parts by the victorious New Republic.<ref name="Chapter 19">{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 19: The Convert}}</ref> Still, in a rejection of the ideals left behind by the Imperial Military, [[Chancellor (New Republic)|Chancellor]] [[Mon Mothma]] championed the [[Military Disarmament Act]] for the New Republic.<ref name="BtCtG">''[[Star Wars: Battles that Changed the Galaxy]]''</ref> The Empire's various remnants made plays at returning to power with surviving equipment, with stormtroopers and TIE fighters seeing continued action in the Outer Rim.<ref name="Chapter 21">{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 21: The Pirate}}</ref> One particular threat to the New Republic came with the [[Shadow Council (Imperial Remnants)|Shadow Council]], which assembled a grouping of seemingly disconnected Imperial remnants on the promise that [[Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] "Thrawn" would soon return and, with his leadership, the Imperial Military would be reborn.<ref name="Chapter 23">{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 23: The Spies}}</ref>

[[File:HuxAndStormtroopersAtCommandCenter-TFAOfficialTeaser.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The First Order disregarded the Galactic Concordance, allowing a new generation of stormtroopers to rise.]]
Over the years, elements of the Imperial armed forces founded the [[First Order]], which remained located in the far corners of the [[Unknown Regions]]<ref name="Before the Awakening">''[[Before the Awakening]]''</ref> as it grew in power.<ref name="Episode VII">{{Film|VII}}</ref> Briefly inhabiting a wing of New Republic politics known as the [[Centrists]], the First Order eventually [[First Order secession|seceded]] from the Republic—a move that was welcomed by many—firmly committed towards reestablishing the former glory of the Galactic Empire.<ref name="TFA VD" /><ref name="Propaganda" /> The regime grew its ranks of [[First Order stormtrooper|new stormtroopers]] by kidnapping and indoctrinating children under [[Project Resurrection]].<ref name="Battlefront II">''[[Star Wars Battlefront II]]''</ref> With the completion of [[Starkiller Base]],<ref name="Episode VII" /> the First Order invaded the galaxy in the opening days of the [[First Order-Resistance War]]<ref name="Episode VIII">{{Film|VIII}}</ref> but lacked the numbers of the Empire that came before it. Hope for the First Order came with the news of Palpatine's survival, which promised the regime a new, massive [[Sith Eternal military|military force]], but Palpatine's forces were destroyed in the [[Battle of Exegol]]. The First Order then collapsed under a [[Uprising against the First Order|galaxy-wide uprising]].<ref name="Episode IX">{{Film|IX}}</ref>

==Organization==
===Organizational breakdown and command===
{{Quote|The Emperor is going to winnow the populations of the galaxy until the only ones left are the ones he can control. And he and Vader and Tarkin are going to accomplish that with an army of steadfast recruits who might as well be clones for the little independent thinking they do, weapons that haven't been seen in more than a thousand years, and ''fear''.|[[Berch Teller]]|<ref name="Tarkin" />}}
[[File:JakeMurrary-TheDiplomat.png|thumb|left|300px|As the de facto Commander-in-Chief and eventually formal supreme commander, Darth Vader was the unofficial-turned-official head of the Imperial Military.]]
The Imperial Military consisted of two main branches: the Imperial Navy and Imperial Army, along with seven smaller branches: the [[Imperial Department of Military Research]], [[Imperial Diplomatic Corps|Diplomatic Corps]], [[Imperial Engineering Corps|Engineering Corps]], [[Imperial Survey Corps|Survey and Exploration Corps]], [[Surgical Corps]], [[Military Police (Galactic Empire)|Military Police]], and [[Imperial Intelligence]].<ref name="DawnVG">''[[Star Wars: Dawn of Rebellion: The Visual Guide]]''</ref> The affairs of the Army and the Navy were overseen by the [[Joint Chiefs of the Imperial Military]], the various commanders of the two branches including the [[Chief of the Imperial Army]] and [[Chief of the Imperial Navy]], while the [[Stormtrooper Corps]] operated under the oversight of the Army.<ref name="Tarkin" /> Supreme command was given to the Galactic Emperor,<ref name="LotS">''[[Lords of the Sith]]''</ref> whose authority was discharged to the acting [[Commander-in-Chief]],<ref name="Bust Collection" /> his [[Sith apprentice|apprentice]] and the so-called [[Emperor's Fist]] [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]], referred to as 1 and 2 respectively.<ref name="LotS" />

However, the responsibilities of the military were not among the attributes of the Emperor's Fist and instead there existed a proper military-ranking [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)|Supreme Commander]] as the next-in-line martial authority.<ref name="Dawn" /> There was also a [[War Ministry]]<ref name="TIE">''[[TIE Fighter Owners' Workshop Manual]]''</ref> led by the [[Minister of War (Galactic Empire)|Minister of War]] responsible for tackling the administrative<ref name="Thrawn">[[Thrawn (novel)|''Thrawn'']]</ref> and financial needs of the military.<ref name="TIE" /> [[Imperial High Command]], which was structured beneath the Joint Chiefs' cabinet, was the central command division for the different military branches<ref name="Lost Stars">''[[Lost Stars]]''</ref><ref name="Heir">''[[Heir to the Jedi]]''</ref> and included the [[Admiralty (Imperial)|Admiralty]], [[General Staff]], [[Stormtrooper Command]], and [[Battle Station Command]].<ref name="In the Name of the Rebellion">{{Rebels|In the Name of the Rebellion}}</ref> In the aftermath of the Battle of Yavin, the military command was reworked around Vader, who was formally named [[supreme commander of the Imperial fleet]].<ref name="Rise" />

===Personnel and enlistment===
{{Quote|Be a part of something. Join the Empire. Explore new worlds. Learn valuable skills. Bring order and unity to the galaxy.|An Imperial recruitment advertisement|<ref name="Solo">''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Arrival-ROTJHD.png|thumb|right|300px|The Galactic Empire utilized its imposing military might in order to secure dominion over the galaxy.]]
Enlistment was open to [[human]] individuals, who were trained at the numerous [[Imperial Academy]] complexes across the galaxy, with many members becoming enlisted personnel, officers or [[stormtrooper]]s.<ref name="Ultimate">''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]''</ref> The Empire had billions of soldiers during its height, the largest military force the galaxy had ever seen. Since the Empire favored humans and [[Xenophobia|looked down]] on [[alien]]s, it was rare to find non-humans serving in the Imperial Military.<ref name="rebelsvisual">''[[Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide]]''</ref><ref name="Epic Battles">''[[Star Wars Rebels: Visual Guide: Epic Battles]]''</ref> The Empire also maintained a [[Veteran Services]] department, which was, at least in theory, established to provide aid to military veterans, including to those who had served in the Republic's armed forces during the Clone Wars. In practice, however, Veteran Services could take so long to provide that aid that it was easier to look for other avenues of help.<ref name="Catalyst" />

Embodying the spirit of the [[Tarkin Doctrine]], the Empire's armed forces relied on the sheer threat of force to keep neighboring systems in line. Fear and intimidation were essential for all Imperial Military operations, exemplified by the sheer size of Star Destroyers, which acted as both a physical and psychological deterrent from assaulting it—and the Empire.<ref name="Tarkin" /> Additionally, the Empire frequently disregarded<ref name="Life Debt">''[[Aftermath: Life Debt]]''</ref> the rules of war<ref name="Timelines">''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''</ref> laid out in the [[Galactic Accord of Systems]].<ref name="Life Debt" />

The Imperial Department of Military Research helped the Imperial Military by upgrading and developing new weapons and technology, while former companies friendly to the Republic helped manufacture the newer Imperial vessels entering production, with some of the largest being [[Sienar Fleet Systems]] and [[Kuat Drive Yards]]. Organizations such as the [[Naval Intelligence Agency]], Imperial Intelligence, and [[Imperial Security Bureau]] often worked in conjunction with the Imperial Military, with the ISB installing an internal affairs officer aboard all ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers and utilizing informants to discover the location of the [[Rebel Alliance Navy|Alliance Fleet]] during the Galactic Civil War.<ref name="Lost Stars" /><ref name="Heir" />
